preamble: The transformation of voiced bilabial stop [b] to the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] is a complex shift involving palatalization, affrication, and devoicing.

examples.note: Bantu labial palatalization can result in voiceless alveolar affricates, often as part of a larger series of consonant mergers.
In some southern Italo-Romance dialects, labial clusters can develop into dental/alveolar affricates.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
